schema.appDescription: Find out exactly when you can retire based on your current savings, monthly investment, and target retirement corpus. Reverse-engineer your FIRE date.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Retirement Timeline Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: How do I calculate when I can retire?
faq.a: Calculate FIRE number (annual expenses ÷ withdrawal rate). Then find how many years it takes for (current corpus + monthly SIP) at your return rate to reach the FIRE number. Use the retirement timeline calculator to get the exact year.

faq.q: How do increases in monthly investment affect retirement age?
faq.a: Doubling your monthly SIP doesn't halve time to retirement — it compresses it by less due to the non-linear nature of compounding. But starting earlier has a dramatic effect: a 25-year-old investing ₹20,000/month at 12% reaches ₹5 crore in 23 years. Starting at 35 requires ₹50,000/month for the same target.

faq.q: What should I do if my retirement date is too far?
faq.a: Three levers: increase income (higher investments), reduce target expenses (lower FIRE number), or accept slightly higher withdrawal rate (3.5% instead of 3%). Also consider semi-retirement — transitioning to lower-pressure consulting work while corpus grows.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹20 lakh corpus, ₹50,000/month investment, 12%
examples.text: FIRE number at ₹1 lakh/month expenses: ₹3 crore (25×). Starting corpus ₹20 lakh + ₹50,000/month at 12%: reaches ₹3 crore in approximately 11.5 years. Retirement at age 43 (if starting at 32).

examples.heading: Effect of increasing monthly investment
examples.text: Same setup but increasing to ₹75,000/month: reaches ₹3 crore in 9.5 years (age 41). Two years saved by adding ₹25,000/month more. Each extra ₹10,000/month cuts retirement by 6–12 months depending on where you are in the accumulation phase.
